ptxt.intxrefpoff: Front Strut Assembly Removal and Installation

attention1.ptxt: Since high-pressure gas is sealed inside front strut (1), there is risk of personal injury if it is handled without observing the following precautions.

item.ptxt: Do not put front strut into fire.
Do not store front strut where it gets hot.

Before disposing of front strut, be sure to drill an approximately 3 mm (0.1 in.) diameter hole (2) in it at point indicated in the figure to let gas out of the cylinder together with oil. Be sure to wear eye shield when drilling hole because metal chips produced by drilling can injure your eyes although the gas itself is harmless.
Check each front strut for oil leakage, damage or deformation. If check result is not OK, replace front strut as an assembly, as it is not repairable.
Inspect front strut function as follows. If check result is not OK, replace front strut as an assembly, because it cannot be disassembled.
Check and adjust tire pressures as specified.
Bounce vehicle body up and down 3 or 4 times continuously by pushing front end of vehicle side body to check front strut.
Also, note how many times vehicle body rebounds to stop after force application.
Repeat the same procedure to the other front strut to confirm that both struts equally function.
If conditions of front struts are in doubt, compare them with known-good vehicle or front strut.
